description_long: This object is a cylindrical guard stone made of light-colored marble with a smooth, polished finish. The front features a sculpted relief of a robed figure, possibly representing a historical or mythological character, set within a shallow niche. The top and bottom edges are adorned with classical architectural molding, and the body is fluted, reminiscent of ancient Greek or Roman columns. The rear and sides are plain, with vertical grooves running the length of the cylinder. The marble surface displays subtle veining and a slightly weathered appearance, suggesting exposure to the elements. This guard stone is both decorative and functional, serving as a protective barrier in urban or historical settings.
description: A marble cylindrical guard stone with classical fluting, ornate molding, and a sculpted relief of a robed figure on the front. The surface is polished with visible marble veining and slight weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing the sculpted relief of a robed figure within a niche and detailed molding at the top and bottom.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the cylindrical shape, fluted grooves, and the protruding relief figure partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying only the fluted marble surface without any sculptural details or ornamentation.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, with the fluted grooves and the edge of the relief figure visible.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the corners of urban streets, near the entrances of historical buildings, or along the edges of plazas to protect structures from vehicle impact.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, and often anchored to a solid base or pavement.
attribute_wear_patterns: Likely to show chipping and smoothing on the lower edges from vehicle contact, as well as surface discoloration and erosion from weather exposure. The relief figure may exhibit more pronounced smoothing on protruding features due to accidental contact or cleaning.
